parsed.final_response: You're really scared that if you open up to your friend, he'll see you as a liability and that will just confirm your self-doubt. It's like you're already imagining the worst-case scenario, and that's a really tough thing to deal with. Can you tell me more about what you think would happen if you did open up to your friend about your anxiety and financial worries?

parsed.final_response: You're feeling anxious about the review and want to limit career talk with your friend to manage your anxiety and sleep better. That's a great idea, and framing it as a temporary focus rather than pulling away might help. To make sure you're both on the same page, you could try saying something like, 'Hey, I need to focus on the review and my budget right now, so can we keep career talk to a minimum for a bit?' This way, you're setting a clear expectation while still being respectful. What do you think might be the most challenging part of having this conversation with your friend?
